title: No rate limiting on chat messages enables flood DoS
description: The receiveChat method (line 60) broadcasts each incoming chat message to all clients in the chat room with no rate limiting. A malicious client can send thousands of messages per second, which are all relayed to every user in the chat room. This amplifies the attack: a single attacker's traffic is multiplied by N connected users. There is no per-client message throttle, no message queue depth limit, and no maximum message length enforcement.
recommendation: Implement per-client rate limiting (e.g., token bucket with ~10 messages/second). Consider also adding a maximum message length (e.g., 8KB) to prevent memory abuse through very large chat messages.
